capacity_evidence.device: one Blackhole chip from a P300c endpoint (TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0)
capacity_evidence.full_attention_dense_accuracy_path_minim: The selected full-attention precision path repeats normalized input across experts and computes separate BF16 gate/up tensors. At S=131072, each is 32*131072*2880*2 = 24159191040 bytes; input+gate+up alone require 72477573120 bytes, before weights, attention, cache, activations, allocator fragmentation, or outputs.
capacity_evidence.stage_scope: Real-weight S=128 qualification requires a layer-aware precision path: sliding layers use composite SDPA plus BF16/HiFi2 sparse prefill, while full-attention layers use FP32 manual attention plus decoder-local BF16 dense expert prefill. The dense path has a hard advertised-context DRAM lower bound of 72.48 GB against 34.18 GB allocator capacity, so this stage advertises 128 supported tokens while retaining the Hugging Face 131072-token model field. A 256-entry BFP8_B cache is separately validated through decode position 128.
capacity_evidence.unprobed_interval: Hardware accuracy and performance from 129 through the HF-advertised 131072-token extent are unsupported in this stage; the selected full-attention accuracy path cannot fit at the target extent by the recorded live-tensor lower bound.

notes: The functional starting point validates real-weight prefill through S=17, and the fused stage validates non-aligned synthetic prefill through S=33. The optimized stage preserves non-aligned S=3/17/33 behavior and real-weight qualifies S=128 for both layer kinds: layer 12 sliding prefill output PCC is 0.990790 and layer 13 full-attention PCC is 0.990509. Their decode-at-position-128 outputs are 0.996168 and 0.994839. The full-attention result needs FP32 manual attention and decoder-local BF16 dense expert prefill; its three unavoidable BF16 expert tensors exceed device allocator capacity by 38.30 GB at S=131072. Therefore current supported context is 128, while hf_advertised_context remains 131072 as model provenance. Routine decode positions 3 through 18, repeated paged updates, trace replay, and a 256-entry cache through position 128 are validated. Current-token K/V remain BF16 before BFP8_B cache writes.

fused_decoder_stage.capacity_change: No supported-context reduction; current_supported_context advances from 17 to the largest fused prefill validation S=33.

optimized_decoder_stage.validated_decode_positions: 3 through 18 and position 128 after a real-weight S=128 prefill
optimized_decoder_stage.cache_storage_change: BFP8_B replaces BF16 for persistent K/V. For this 1x8x128x64 cache pair, the nominal payload drops from 262144 BF16 bytes to 131072 BFP8 mantissa bytes plus block-exponent metadata.
optimized_decoder_stage.capacity_change: Real-weight S=128 now passes for both layer kinds. The selected full-attention precision path needs at least 72477573120 live BF16 expert-tensor bytes at S=131072 versus 34178731008 allocator bytes, before weights and other state, so current supported context is 128 with limiting_reason=device_dram. The Hugging Face advertised field remains 131072 and is not rewritten.

emit_provenance.collective_collapse: Full Hugging Face weights replace emitted shards; attention and expert all-reduces plus routing mesh partition are absent from runtime.

dtype_policy.functional_baseline: BF16 weights, activations, and cache; TILE layout; DRAM by default
dtype_policy.exception: The emitted router promotes its activation and linear result to FP32 before BF16 top-k; decode heads use one workload-derived L1 height shard.
dtype_policy.optimized_policy: Decode uses BF16 activations/residuals/norms/router/attention weights, BFP8_B routed expert weights with LoFi sparse matmuls, and BFP8_B persistent K/V. Sliding prefill uses BF16/HiFi2 sparse expert outputs; full-attention S=128 uses FP32 manual attention and decoder-local BF16 dense experts. Current-token K/V remain BF16 before cache writes.
dtype_policy.deferred: Complete full-model datatype-frontier selection remains a later datatype-sweep stage.
